author: @Cooliemasteroz
text:
I don't no what your reasons are for having a mechanical speed controller, maybe like me you like to minimise the use of electronics in case of a another Carrington event.
I imagine it would be easy to rig up a pressure vessel and turbine with a throttle control to drive it.
I've been thinking it would be a good idea as a back up in pressurised mars rovers for manned missions.

author: @DavidValinGSense
text: Do Not Miss Next 5 videos, wait till you see where this is going... Bravo... Lateral Thinking at it's best... Watch what comes out of this Brilliant New way of Thinking, Frequency... Light... then Gravity... Einstein in the room... Bravo!

author: @stevecummins324
text:
Especially if there's lots of comments, is understandable to not reply to many.

Shaping of tips. Have you heard of the feynman sprinkler. Water jet spraying out from a wheel results in torque which turns the  wheel, but if flow reversed will it still turn? Intuition often says yes. But does it in practice.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that's a bit like the Hero engine isn't it? So, I would guess yes too

replies.author: @stevecummins324
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering yes like the steam powered  aeropile. Or water pressure powered turbine equivalent a segnor wheel. My intuition was also they would be reversible, but  they only turn well when fluid get flung out. OK Sometimes there's a tiny amount of spin when fluid flowing the other way, but it takes exceptionally good bearings for such to happen.

Kind of makes it even more interesting that motors and generators can usually be reversed

author: @kevinleebailey
text: You could make a magnetic reed switching system to run the motor.

replies.author: @kevinleebailey
replies.text: A piece of bendy brass or copper with a magnet soldered on and another to make the circuit. The magnet will attract to the metal on the rotor making and breaking the circuit you would need two of these in just the right place to make the motor work.

replies.author: @kevinleebailey
replies.text: Just like a pulse motor but the reed switch has the magnet inside its self . A magnetically assisted reed switch ?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: for sure mate

author: @brettmoore3194
text: Just take your mains . Put diodes on each phase then neutral because younwound it Y style. It would pulse dc in different polarity. By the way you described it its a drag style magnetic motor instead of a push. Always wondered which way worked best

replies.author: @stev838
replies.text:
Yes push is better.   Disc timer    Diodes 
11000.  Rpm.   Add a fly wheel and your done

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice idea mate but not with this one - it's a model for checking concepts and is basically held together with superglue - for something faster I will have to rebuild it stronger - but I didn't want to do that first time as it costs a lot in time and money and if I got it wrong well - that would be annoying lol

replies.author: @stev838
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering yes  I tried to build one a few years ago.   I stretched it horizontally     Like the spiral heart  pump
I found that you have to angle the solenoids to create a leading edge  I took the charge created by the passing of the inner magnets  energizing the solenoids  and routed that the the next solenoid 
A disc on the drive shaft controlled the timing of the charge  so it pushed its self 
Over and over.    You had to kick start it 
But it continued to spin.   Push was better than pull    And diodes would have been the next thing  it was crude. But would spin many more times than gravity allows.   Almost had it.     But with wood and glue and magnets you can imagine how it would all shift around.  For the inner race of magnets I used a spiral brush from a carpet cleaner.   And little magnets glued to it.   Keep at it it’s does what you want it to do.     A real one would have to be governed with load.  It likes to run up.  N ver added the fly wheel 
But that’s the secret     A self assisting dc  spiral motor.     I called it the hyperdine 
Load equal to weight and velocity of fly wheel I believe.  But with a fluid transmission   A hundred pound model would power a dump truck    Nice job

replies.author: @stev838
replies.text:
I guess best described as a 
phase generator

author: @wmffgeorge1589
text: I think after playing that song so meny times I'm starting to become a bit iratated by it. So the strips elongate the magnetic field to one side making the motor turn one way? I'm strugling to picture that in my mind a bit.

replies.author: @stevecummins324
replies.text:
How to explain motion... 
a ball and round bottom bowl.
Place bowl right way up.. Drop ball in anywhere.. It'll roll towards central point... Aka stable equilibrium.
Put bowl upside down. If true Bowl shape (no flat bottom) . Ball could in theory be placed on bottom with no tenancy to roll... But in practice slightlet disturbance or misplacement and ball will start moving more and more. In direction it started moving... Unstable equilibrium.

Knowingly place ball slightly off to one side of bowl bass. It's gonna want to roll away from center

replies.author: @wmffgeorge1589
replies.text: @stevecummins324 yes well I somewhat understood that from the start, but I'm strugling to picture how the magnetic fields ar more imbalanced this way, uspeshaly to one direction.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it either irritates you or grows on you mate - personally I use It because it is royalty free and I am a cheapskate lol
